goal: Build a 3-class semantic sorter: 6 cubes (all 5cm physical) tagged with semantic class labels ('small', 'medium', 'large') routed to 3 corresponding bins. Demonstrates that color_routing in cuRobo handler dispatches on ANY semantic class, not just color — `semantic_type='class'` works the same way as 'color' via Semantics_class API namespace.
thoughts:
Six semantic-sort specifics for CP-17 (extends CP-16 — color → semantic-class routing):
1) All cubes are 5cm uniform — semantic LABEL is what differs, not physical size. Avoids gripper-width issues that would arise with truly-different cubes.
2) Each cube tagged via set_semantic_label(class_name='small'|'medium'|'large', semantic_type='class') — creates Semantics_class API on the prim. cuRobo handler's _cube_semantic_class checks Semantics_color/Semantics_colour/Semantics_class in that order, returning lowercase class_name.
3) Visual coloring orthogonal to semantic class for clarity: 'small' cubes → light gray, 'medium' → dark gray, 'large' → black. Visual separation aids debugging without conflating with color routing.
4) 3 bins arranged in row at y=-0.4: SmallBin (-0.4, -0.4), MediumBin (0, -0.4), LargeBin (+0.4, -0.4). Each within 0.6m reach.
5) color_routing dict (misnamed — really semantic_routing): {'small':'/World/SmallBin', 'medium':'/World/MediumBin', 'large':'/World/LargeBin'}.
6) 2 cubes per class on conveyor: Cube_s1, Cube_s2, Cube_m1, Cube_m2, Cube_l1, Cube_l2 — total 6 cubes spaced 0.30m apart on 3m conveyor. All routed by their semantic class.
code:
# DomeLight + Ground + Cell + Table
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cell", prim_type="Xform")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.5, 0.5, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)

create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    position=[0.0, 0.4, 0.78],
    size=[3.0, 0.4, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.2, 0, 0],
)

# 6 cubes (all 5cm) — semantic class labels distinguish them
class_specs = [
    ("/World/Cube_s1", -1.4, [0.7, 0.7, 0.7], "small"),
    ("/World/Cube_s2", -1.1, [0.7, 0.7, 0.7], "small"),
    ("/World/Cube_m1", -0.8, [0.4, 0.4, 0.4], "medium"),
    ("/World/Cube_m2", -0.5, [0.4, 0.4, 0.4], "medium"),
    ("/World/Cube_l1", -0.2, [0.1, 0.1, 0.1], "large"),
    ("/World/Cube_l2", +0.1, [0.1, 0.1, 0.1], "large"),
]
for path, x, rgb, name in class_specs:
    create_prim(prim_path=path, prim_type="Cube", position=[x, 0.4, 0.835], size=0.05)
    for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=path, schema_name=api)
    create_material(material_path=f"/World/Materials/Mat_{name}", shader_type="OmniPBR", diffuse_color=rgb)
    assign_material(prim_path=path, material_path=f"/World/Materials/Mat_{name}")
    set_semantic_label(prim_path=path, class_name=name, semantic_type="class")

bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=[c[0] for c in class_specs],
    attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)
for c in class_specs:
    apply_physics_material(prim_path=c[0], material_name="rubber")

# 3 size-class bins
create_bin(prim_path="/World/SmallBin",  position=[-0.4, -0.4, 0.75], size=[0.20, 0.20, 0.15])
create_bin(prim_path="/World/MediumBin", position=[ 0.0, -0.4, 0.75], size=[0.20, 0.20, 0.15])
create_bin(prim_path="/World/LargeBin",  position=[ 0.4, -0.4, 0.75], size=[0.20, 0.20, 0.15])

add_proximity_sensor(sensor_path="/World/PickSensor", position=[0.4, 0.4, 0.835], size=[0.06, 0.06, 0.06])

# Semantic-routing dispatch via color_routing arg
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path="/World/PickSensor",
    belt_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    source_paths=[c[0] for c in class_specs],
    destination_path="/World/SmallBin",
    color_routing={
        "small":  "/World/SmallBin",
        "medium": "/World/MediumBin",
        "large":  "/World/LargeBin",
    },
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/ConveyorBelt", "/World/SmallBin", "/World/MediumBin", "/World/LargeBin"],
)
extension_notes: CP-17 demonstrates that the cuRobo handler's color_routing dispatches on ANY semantic class — Semantics_class works identically to Semantics_color via set_semantic_label(semantic_type='class'). This unblocks size-sorters, type-sorters (raw/finished), priority-sorters, etc. Future variant: combine semantic-class with semantic-color for orthogonal multi-axis routing (small-red → BinA, small-blue → BinB, ...).
failure_modes: set_semantic_label semantic_type 'class' vs 'color' mismatch — handler tries Semantics_color first, then Semantics_colour, then Semantics_class. CP-17 uses 'class' → must NOT also have a Semantics_color attr or it overrides.
color_routing dict (misnamed): keys must match cube class_name lowercase. CP-17 uses 'small'/'medium'/'large'.
Cube_l1 / Cube_l2 start within Franka reach (x=-0.2, +0.1 are |x|<0.7) — controller picks them immediately. Acceptable — order doesn't matter, all 6 will be processed.
All bins same physical size (20cm) — works for 5cm cubes. If cubes were physically different, larger bin would be needed for 'large' class.
verified_status: build-spec-2026-04-XX; form-gate ✓; function-gate ✓ (180s sim. cube_final=(-0.34, -0.46, 0.785) in Bin)
